Formula: FAAB the winning manager spent to land this waiver claim. = the winning claim's waiver_bid; 0 for free-agent / commissioner adds.
Notes: Populated only on waiver rows.

Formula: Total FAAB the whole league bid on this player this week. Sum of every claim's waiver_bid, winning and losing.
Notes: The winning bid sits on top of this stack.

Formula: How much the winning bid beat the next-highest valid bid. winning_bid − max(other valid bids ≤ winning_bid).
Notes: Bids above the winning bid were invalid (roster full / short on FAAB) and are ignored. Blank for uncontested waivers.

Formula: The winner's markup over the runner-up, as a share of the winning bid (so it's comparable across big and small bids). (winning_bid − runner_up) / winning_bid × 100.
Notes: 0–100; 100 against a $0 runner-up. Blank with no valid runner-up.

Formula: How many managers tried to claim this player this week. Count of every waiver attempt, winning and losing.
Notes: Measures interest, not wins.

Formula: Fantasy points per game the added player produced while on this team. Mean nflverse PPR points over NFL games from the pickup Date to the next drop/trade (open-ended if still rostered).
Notes: Every week in the window counts, started or benched. Falls back to player_week when nflverse has no record.

Stat: Average PPG of dropped player over same time
Formula: The dropped player's points per game over that same window — what you'd have gotten by keeping him. Mean nflverse points from pickup to the added player's next drop/trade.
Notes: Blank with no dropped player or no games in the window.

Formula: The added player's recent form just before you got him. Mean points over his 5 most-recent PLAYED NFL games before the pickup (fewer than 5 → averages what exists).
How much more the added player scored than the dropped one over the added player's tenure. Average PPG on team − Average PPG of dropped player over same time.
Notes: A missing side counts as 0.

Stat: Difference of averages adjusted by position
Formula: Same added-minus-dropped PPG gap, but each side scaled for its position so a 12-PPG TE isn't judged like a 12-PPG QB. Each PPG × (league starter avg / position avg).
Notes: Position averages are all-time starter averages from player_week.

Formula: Age gap between the added and dropped players. added age − dropped age in decimal years, at the pickup date.
Notes: Negative = the team got younger.

Formula: Was this pickup a handcuff to a stud the team already had? TRUE if the team still rosters a same-NFL-team, same-position STARTER (started in the pickup week or the 2 before) who averaged 10+ more PPG over the last 5 played games.
Notes: The stud must still be rostered at pickup to count.

Formula: How many weeks the added player sat on the bench before his first start for this team. Count of rostered non-starter weeks between the pickup and that first start.
Notes: Blank if he was dropped/traded before ever starting.

Stat: Number of starts before next drop
Formula: How many weeks the added player started for this team before leaving it. Count of started weeks between the pickup and the next drop/trade.
Notes: If never dropped, counts starts through the end of the data.

Formula: How long the added player stayed on this team, in days. Pickup Date to the next drop/trade off this team, or to today if still rostered.
Notes: Blank on a pure drop (no added player); a same-day add-and-move stays 0.

Formula: Share of his rostered weeks the added player actually started. Number of starts before next drop / weeks rostered over the tenure.
Notes: Bye and injury weeks stay in the denominator (gross start rate).

Stat: Injury adjusted % of starts made while rostered
Formula: Start rate counting only weeks he was available. Like '% of starts made while rostered' but drops bye/injury weeks from both top and bottom.

Overall grade of the pickup: how much it out-scored the drop, amplified by how often the player actually started, plus a handcuff bonus. (Difference of averages adjusted by position) × (1 + % of starts) × (1 + injury-adjusted % of starts) + 5 if Cuff at time of pickup?.
Notes: 0 if the added player was never rostered a full NFL week; blank on a pure drop.

Formula: Fantasy points the added player put up in the weeks he STARTED for this team. Summed from pickup to his next drop/trade; 0 if no add.
Notes: Started weeks come from player_week.

Formula: What the dropped player scored over exactly the weeks the added player started — the cost of starting the add instead. His real NFL points (0 for any bye/injury/DNP week); 0 if no drop or no add.
Notes: Counted from his game log wherever he actually landed.

Formula: Net started-week production the swap gained the team. Points Added − Points Lost; 0 for a pure drop.
Notes: Positive = the add out-scored the player it replaced.

Formula: Points Added per started week — comparable across swaps of different lengths. Points Added / started weeks (0 when none).
Points Lost per started week. Points Lost / started weeks (0 when none).
Net points per started week. Net points / started weeks (0 when none).

Stat: Avg points added adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points added scaled for the added player's position, so cross-position swaps compare. Each week × (league starter avg / position avg) before averaging.

Stat: Avg points lost adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points lost scaled for the dropped player's position. Each week × (league starter avg / position avg) before averaging.

Stat: Avg net points adjusted by position
Formula: Position-normalised net of the swap. Avg points added adjusted by position − Avg points lost adjusted by position.

Stat: KTC value of player added at deal time
Formula: The added player's dynasty trade value on the pickup date. KeepTradeCut superflex value (sf_trade_value).
Notes: Superflex (2QB league); KTC history starts ≈ April 2021. Blank before that or for an untracked player.

Stat: KTC value of player dropped at deal time
Formula: The dropped player's KTC superflex value on the transaction date.
Notes: Blank for a pure add, an untracked player, or a pre-April-2021 date.

Stat: Net KTC value at deal time
Formula: Dynasty value the team gained or lost on the swap, at deal time. KTC of added − KTC of dropped; a missing side counts as 0.
Notes: Superflex values.

Stat: KTC value of player added at end of season
Formula: The added player's KTC superflex value at this season's end (Monday after the championship).
Notes: Blank for a future season end or pre-history date.

Stat: KTC value of player added 1 year later
Formula: The added player's KTC superflex value one year after the transaction date.
Notes: Blank when that date is still in the future.

Stat: KTC value of player added 2 years later
Formula: The added player's KTC superflex value two years after the transaction date.
Notes: Blank when that date is still in the future.

Stat: KTC value of player dropped at end of season
Formula: The dropped player's KTC superflex value at this season's end — did dropping him turn out to be a mistake? Monday after the championship.
Notes: Blank for a future/pre-history date or a pure add.

Stat: KTC value of player dropped 1 year later
Formula: The dropped player's KTC superflex value one year after the transaction date.
Notes: Blank when still in the future or a pure add.

Stat: KTC value of player dropped 2 years later
Formula: The dropped player's KTC superflex value two years after the transaction date.
Notes: Blank when still in the future or a pure add.

Stat: Net KTC value at end of season
Formula: Dynasty value the team was ahead/behind by season's end. KTC of added − KTC of dropped at season end; missing side = 0.
Notes: Superflex values.

Formula: KTC of added − KTC of dropped one year after the deal; missing side = 0.
Notes: Superflex values.

Formula: KTC of added − KTC of dropped two years after the deal; missing side = 0.
Notes: Superflex values.

Formula: The player this move added to the team (waiver claim, free-agent, or commissioner add). Blank on a pure-drop row.
Notes: Links to the player's player_all_time row.

Formula: The player this move dropped from the team (cut for room, or the FA-drop side). Blank when nothing was dropped.
Notes: Links to the player's player_all_time row.

Formula: How the add happened: a WAIVER claim (FAAB bid), a FREE-AGENT pickup (no bid), or a COMMISSIONER add.
Notes: FAAB columns are populated for waivers only.

Formula: The date this team later dropped or traded the added player away. First such date from the movement log.
Notes: Blank if still rostered or the row wasn't an add.

Formula: Clickable jump to the added player's NEXT move anywhere in the league. Row pointer: '#N' = transactions row, 'T#N' = trades row, 'PH#N' = picks row.
Notes: A multi-row trade counts as one event. Blank at the end of the chain or with no added player.

Formula: Clickable jump to the added player's PREVIOUS move (same '#N' / 'T#N' / 'PH#N' scheme). A drafted player's chain starts at his picks draft row.
Notes: Blank at the start of the chain or with no added player.

Formula: Clickable jump to the dropped player's NEXT move anywhere in the league (same scheme).
Notes: Blank at the end of the chain or with no dropped player.

Formula: Clickable jump to the dropped player's PREVIOUS move anywhere in the league (same scheme).
Notes: Blank at the start of the chain or with no dropped player.

Stat: Number of times picked up by this team
Formula: How many times this team has acquired this player, counting up to now. Includes trades-in; 2nd acquisition reads 2.
Notes: Ordered by date across adds and trades.

Stat: Number of times dropped by this team
Formula: How many times this team has let this player go, counting up to now. Includes trades-away; 2nd departure reads 2.
Notes: Blank on a row that dropped no one (a pure pickup).

Formula: How much this single move nudged the team's tank score: positive = younger and/or richer in future picks (tanking), negative = win-now. (1/6)×(age change) + (1/9)×(future-pick change) — the only two tank terms a roster move can touch. Age change rewards swapping in younger players/picks (vs the team's roster age that week); pick change round-weights future picks received minus sent (1st .25, 2nd .09, 3rd .03, 4th .01; next 3 seasons only).
Notes: Waiver/FA moves shift no picks, so their pick term is 0. Picks are aged as future rookies.

Formula: Everything that came TO this team in the trade: players, draft picks ('2025 1.05(B. Robinson)'), and FAAB ('$N FAAB'). Semicolon-joined; FAAB is listed per sender, so a 3-team deal shows '$4 FAAB' + '$15 FAAB' rather than one lumped '$19 FAAB'.
Notes: A future pick shows its original owner ('2027 2(Oliverwkw)'); a made pick shows the player it became.

Formula: Everything this team GAVE UP in the trade, each attributed to the roster that actually gave it up — so in a 3+ team deal each asset is listed once per side, not claimed by every team. Semicolon-joined; FAAB summed per sending roster.
Notes: Net-zero swaps (nothing really changed hands) are dropped from trades.csv and all counts.

Formula: How many players and picks this team received in the trade. Counts 'Assets received' entries; FAAB is money, not an asset, so it's excluded.
Notes: A FAAB-only haul reads 0.

Formula: How many players and picks this team gave up in the trade. Counts 'Assets sent' entries; FAAB excluded.
Notes: A FAAB-only give-up reads 0.

Stat: Total number of assets in trade
Formula: Total players and picks that changed hands in the WHOLE trade, counting each once no matter who sent or received it. FAAB excluded.
Notes: Same value on every row of a multi-team trade (the whole pot), not just this row's received + sent.

Formula: Overall grade of how much a trade helped the team, dominated by games it actually swung. Weighted sum of five z-scored signals, scaled to read like an added-win total (≈ −4..+16 league-wide, most within ±2): WIN IMPACT ×2.0 + Avg net points ×0.8 + Trade addition value ×0.5 + Pick value received ×0.5 + (−Asset difference in average age) ×0.3. WIN IMPACT = games the trade flipped (weeks where the received starters vs the best players given up would have changed the win/loss), plus a value-weighted share of games flipped by later trades that reused these assets.
Notes: Renamed from 'Team performance improvement'. Uses nflverse points + team_week margins.

Formula: A single 0–100 grade for the move (~50 average, 80+ strong): average of FOUR key stats, each turned into its percentile across that sheet's rows. The four — transactions: Avg net points, Player addition value, latest KTC, % of starts made while rostered; trades: Avg net points, Trade addition value, latest KTC, Trade impact score; picks: Avg points added, pick-adjusted Player addition value, latest pick-adjusted KTC, pick-adjusted Avg career PPG adjusted by position. 'Latest KTC' = the most recent populated KTC checkpoint on the row. A value of exactly 0 (no production) ranks at the bottom of its tie so zeros cluster low.
Notes: Pure drops get a separate 0–50 score (half-scaled so a perfect drop never out-scores a good add). Blank for vet/unmade picks, one-sided untracked trades, and untracked players. Percentiles are within each sheet.

Formula: Points-per-game the dropped player put up after you cut him — negated, so more negative = worse drop. Mean NFL points over his next 17 PLAYED games from the drop date (fewer so far → averages what exists; a real 0 = never played again).
Notes: Blank only when nothing was dropped.

Formula: Total points the dropped player put up over those next 17 played games — negated (more negative = more given away). Same window as Dropped avg points.
Notes: Blank only when nothing was dropped.

Stat: KTC value difference at deal time
Formula: Which team won the deal's dynasty value on day one. Depth-adjusted KTC received − sent, superflex. DEPTH TAX: on each side the best asset counts full and each next is discounted (2nd ×0.6, 3rd ×0.6², …), since you can only start so many — so a 3-scrubs-for-1-stud package is taxed while a 1-for-1 is the raw difference. FAAB counts at the league-avg KTC-per-$.
Notes: Covers players, picks, and FAAB. Positive = this team won the deal.

Stat: KTC value difference at end of season
Formula: The same depth-adjusted received − sent KTC gap, re-checked at this season's end (Monday after the championship) — how the deal looked once the season played out.
Notes: Superflex, depth-taxed. Future/pre-history dates are blank.

Formula: The depth-adjusted received − sent KTC gap one year after the trade — who's ahead a year on.
Notes: Superflex, depth-taxed. Blank when still in the future.

Formula: The depth-adjusted received − sent KTC gap two years after the trade — the long-run verdict on who won.
Notes: Superflex, depth-taxed. Blank when still in the future.

Formula: How much draft-pick value this team brought in — highlights pick-heavy hauls. Σ KTC of just the picks received at deal time (players excluded).
Notes: Blank when no picks were received.

Stat: Change in pick value at draft time
Formula: Whether the received picks drew a better or worse slot than expected at the trade. For each unresolved pick received: KTC once it became a rookie (Sept 1 of its draft year) − KTC at deal time, summed.
Notes: Picks whose drafts are still in the future don't contribute.

Formula: Of the assets received in this trade, those the team still holds today (never traded out or dropped to FA). Picks show as 'YYYY R.SLOT(F. Last)' once drafted.
Of the assets received in this trade, those it later moved on in another TRADE (rather than dropping to FA). Whichever exit came first decides.
Of the assets received in this trade, those it later DROPPED to free agency rather than trading. Picks excluded (picks can't be dropped).
Notes: From this team's later 'Player Dropped' entries after the trade date.

Formula: What this trade's haul turned into one hop later: the received side of the next trade(s) where the team gave up any asset from this haul.
Notes: Picks resolve to slot + drafted player where known, e.g. '2024 1.05(B. Robinson)'.

Stat: Additional assets traded away in those deals
Formula: The extra cost of converting the haul: other assets given up alongside it in those immediate-next trades.
Notes: Dedups across multiple downstream hops.

Stat: Return from trades of trades...of trades. Keep going until present day
Formula: Everything this trade's haul eventually became, following each asset through every later trade to the present.
Notes: Picks display with their drafted player when known.

Formula: Whether the trade made the team younger or older — negative = younger. mean(received asset ages) − mean(sent asset ages) at the trade date. Players use birth_date; picks count as future rookies (aged ~21-22, so further-out picks are younger).
Notes: Reports 0 when one side has no aged asset (e.g. FAAB-only).

Formula: How many teams were in the trade — this team plus every counterparty. 2 for a normal swap, 3+ for a multi-team deal.

Stat: Link to next transaction per asset
Formula: For each asset RECEIVED (in 'Assets received' order), a clickable jump to that asset's NEXT event — '#N' transactions, 'T#N' trades, 'PH#N' picks — as a ';'-joined list aligned 1:1. Players follow the player chain; picks follow their pick chain and stop at the draft row; FAAB is blank.
Notes: In the xlsx, exploded into one clickable column per received asset.

Stat: Link to previous transaction per asset
Formula: For each asset RECEIVED, a clickable jump to that asset's PREVIOUS event (same '#N' / 'T#N' / 'PH#N' scheme), ';'-joined 1:1. Built from the received side, so a trade's two mirror rows don't link to each other.
Notes: In the xlsx, exploded into one clickable column per received asset.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Team's average age counting future draft picks as well as rostered players — so a team hoarding picks reads younger (the tank signal). Mean age over rostered players + held future picks that week; picks are aged as future rookies (~21-22). Year/all-time/league average the weekly values.
Notes: Feeds the tanking calculation in place of the rostered-only age.

Stat: Avg PPG of received players on team
Formula: Points per game the received side actually produced for this team. Each received player's mean points from the trade to his next exit, averaged; a received pick counts the player drafted with it over his tenure here (only if this team made the pick).
Notes: nflverse game log, so only games played count. A not-yet-drafted or flipped pick contributes nothing.

Stat: Avg PPG of sent players over same time
Formula: What the sent side would have given you over the same span — each sent player's mean points across the received players' collective tenure, averaged.

Stat: Avg PPG of received players in 5 games before trade
Formula: The received players' recent form just before the trade. Each one's mean points over his 5 most-recent NFL games before the trade date, averaged (fewer than 5 → what's available).
How much better the received side produced per game than the side given up, over the forward-looking window. Avg PPG of received players on team − Avg PPG of sent players over same time.
Notes: Positive = the received players out-scored the sent players.

Stat: Difference of averages adjusted by position
Formula: The same received − sent PPG gap, but each side scaled for position so a cross-position trade is judged on positional value. Each PPG × (league starter avg / position avg).

Overall grade of a trade for this team, mirroring the transaction 'Player addition value' plus a pick term. (Difference of averages adjusted by position) × (1 + received players' % of starts) × (1 + their injury-adjusted % of starts) + 5 if any received player was a handcuff + a future-pick term (received − sent picks, round-weighted like tanking and scaled so a future 1st ≈ +5).
Notes: Pick term counts only next-3-season capital and applies even to a pick-only haul; players drafted with received picks feed the PPG side.

Formula: Points the received side put up in weeks it started for this team. Each week, sum the points of every received asset that STARTED (received players + players drafted with received picks).
Notes: Started weeks/points come from player_week.

Formula: The best plays you forwent: each week with k received starters, the points of the top-k players traded away that week (sent picks count the player drafted with them).
Notes: Counterfactual points from the nflverse game log; the top-k rule generalizes the 1-for-1 transaction Points Lost.

Formula: Net started-week production the deal gained this team. Points added − Points lost.
Notes: Positive = the received side out-produced the best-k sent assets.

Formula: Points added per matched week (weeks with ≥1 received starter). Points added / matched weeks.
Points lost per matched week. Points lost / matched weeks.
Net points per matched week. Net points / matched weeks.

Stat: Avg points added adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points added with each received asset's points scaled for its position, so positions compare. Each × (league starter avg / position avg) before summing.

Stat: Avg points lost adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points lost with each sent asset's points scaled for position. Keeps the same top-k assets (chosen by raw points), then sums their position-adjusted points.

Stat: Avg net points adjusted by position
Formula: Position-normalised net of the deal. Avg points added adjusted by position − Avg points lost adjusted by position.
The pick's 'round.slot' label in DRAFT ORDER (e.g. '1.05' = 5th pick of round 1). Snake drafts (2021 rookie) reverse even rounds; a future pick shows '2.??' until its season drafts.
Notes: Synthetic draft-day picks: '2.09' = the toilet-bracket reward (2024+); '5.01, 5.02, …' = the 20-FAAB draft-day buys (2025+). For slot comparisons 2.09 counts as 2.08 and each 5.0X as 4.08.

Formula: The team that originally owned this pick before any trades — the team in that draft slot per Sleeper. For a traded pick, the chain's origin.
Notes: Distinct from 'Team'. ESPN-era picks fall back to the slot owner, else the picker.

Sheet: player_week / team_week / team_year / team_all_time / transactions / trades / picks
Formula: The team that actually MADE the selection (the last owner in the trade chain). Equals Original Team when the pick wasn't traded.
Notes: For 2021 rookie-draft even rounds (corrupt Sleeper data), repaired from who first rostered the drafted player.

Formula: How long the drafted player stayed on the team that drafted him, in days. Draft day (≈ Aug 28) to his next exit off that team, or to today if still rostered.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick; every made pick is ≥ 0 (an unmappable player falls back to 0).

Formula: Fantasy points per game the drafted player produced for the team that drafted him. Mean points over the games he played from draft day to his next exit off that team (or today).
Notes: Blank if he was never rostered here for an NFL week (or an unmade pick); 0 if rostered but logged no games.

Stat: Avg PPG on team adjusted by position
Formula: Avg PPG on team scaled for position, so a 12-PPG TE isn't judged like a 12-PPG QB. × (league starter avg / position avg).
Notes: Blank / 0 exactly when Avg PPG on team is.

Formula: How the pick panned out overall: the drafted player's points per game across all his NFL games from the draft onward (every team, not just the drafter). Vets count only post-draft games.
Notes: 0 for a made pick whose player never played post-draft; blank only for an unmade pick. nflverse coverage is 2021+.

Stat: Avg career PPG adjusted by position
Formula: Avg career PPG scaled for position. × (league starter avg / position avg).
Notes: Blank / 0 exactly when Avg career PPG is.

Formula: How old the player a pick became was on draft day. Age in decimal years at the draft anchor (≈ Aug 28), from birth_date.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a player with no birth_date.

Formula: Overall grade of the pick, like the transactions version but with no 'dropped' side. (Avg PPG on team adjusted by position) × (1 + % of starts made while rostered by drafting team) × (1 + injury-adjusted % of starts) + 5 if Cuff when drafted?.
Notes: Blank when the player never played an NFL week for the drafter, or for an unmade pick.

Formula: Was the pick a handcuff to a stud the team already had? TRUE if, at the player's first week on the drafting team, it already rostered a same-NFL-team, same-position STARTER averaging 10+ more PPG over the last 5 played games.
Notes: Judged at his first rostered week (a rookie's NFL team/position isn't known until he plays). Feeds Player addition value.

Formula: How many weeks the drafted player sat on the drafting team's roster before his first start for them.
Notes: Blank if he never started for the drafter (or an unmade pick).

Stat: Number of starts before next transaction
Formula: How many weeks the drafted player started for the drafting team before leaving it (trade/drop).
Notes: ≥ 0 for every made pick (0 if he never started); blank for an unmade pick.

Stat: % of starts made while rostered by drafting team
Formula: Share of his rostered weeks the drafted player started for the drafting team. starts / weeks rostered over the post-draft tenure.
Notes: Blank if never rostered an NFL week here; 0 if rostered but never started.

Stat: Injury adjusted % of starts made while rostered by drafting team
Formula: Start rate for the drafting team counting only weeks he was available. starts / rostered weeks, both excluding injury/bye weeks.
Notes: Blank when there were no injury-free rostered weeks (or an unmade pick).

Formula: Points the drafted player put up in the weeks he STARTED for the drafting team. Summed over the post-draft tenure window.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick; ≥ 0 for every made pick.

Formula: Points added per started week. Points added / started weeks for this team.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick; 0 for a made pick with no starts.

Stat: Avg points added adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points added scaled for position. Each started week's points × (league starter avg / position avg), then / started weeks.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick; 0 for a made pick with no starts.

Formula: TRUE when this pick changed owners outside a normal recorded trade — the pick belongs to a non-original owner but no trade event explains the move (typical for picks moved beyond Sleeper's ~3-year window).
Notes: Such picks aren't added to trades.csv; assumed a single move from original to current owner.

Formula: How many times the pick changed hands by trade — recorded Sleeper trades plus off-platform commissioner moves the commissioner re-applied by hand.
Notes: The full ownership lineage lives in the hover-comment on the pick's first cell. Award/non-tradeable picks (2020 startup, 2021 vet draft, 2.09 reward, 5.xx buy) count 0.

Formula: Clickable jump to the drafted PLAYER's first move after the draft ('#N' transaction or 'T#N' trade). The draft row starts his player chain.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a player with no post-draft events.

Formula: Clickable jump to the PICK's last trade before the draft ('T#N'). The draft row ends the pick's trade chain.
Notes: Blank for an unmade or never-traded pick.

Sheet: transactions / trades / all transaction & trade counts
Formula: A single-day commissioner correction that leaves a roster exactly as it started is dropped entirely. Triggered when every player moved nets to zero on its roster within one calendar day and a commissioner action was involved.
Notes: Covers commissioner add+drop, same-day re-adds, undone adds, and reversed trades. Excluded from every transaction/trade count and detail.

Formula: The player's real NFL team that week. Resolved in order: nflverse week stats → season stats → weekly roster (catches IR/suspended/PUP players with no stats) → the sentinel 'NFL' only if he had no roster at all that season (free agent / retired). Team DSTs and unmapped players keep Sleeper's team.
Notes: A player on the 'NFL' sentinel is never flagged on bye (no schedule).

Formula: TRUE when a handcuff actually had to start. This player STARTED, his own last-5 average is under 10, and a same-NFL-team/same-position teammate who averages 10+ more PPG is injured/suspended.
Notes: The hurt teammate needn't have been a starter. Drives 'Number of cuffs started'.

Formula: Whether starting this player was the right call. His points − the highest-scoring bench player who could have started instead.
Notes: Negative = a benched player out-scored him (bad call).

Formula: For a bench player: his points − the weakest starter he could have replaced. Positive = he should have played.

How hard a team is tanking: high when it under-scores the league while stockpiling young players and draft picks. Weekly average of five 0-1 signals — below-league PF, below-league Max PF, young roster (incl. picks), this year's picks, and future draft capital. team_year = the final week's value; team_all_time = mean of seasons.
Notes: PF/age signals are season-to-date. Never summed across weeks (each value is already a running average).

Formula: How lucky or unlucky a team's result was, given how it and its opponent actually played — rewards unlucky losses / strong-but-beaten weeks, penalizes lucky wins. Weekly composite of: beating (or missing) a pregame talent estimate, the opponent collapsing or your studs popping (counted only when the game was close), a heavy penalty for injuries/byes, plus efficiency, close-game and bad-beat terms and an extra hit when hurt starters cost a winnable game. team_year Luck = sum of weekly Luck; team_all_time 'Avg yearly luck' = mean of the season totals.
Notes: Calibrated so winning to your talent nets ≈ 0; each loss-from-hardship week subtracts 0.25. All-time averages seasons (not weeks) to stay fair across tenures. Full weighting in plan/LUCK_REWORK.md.

Formula: Fantasy points a team lost to injury/suspension that week. Sum, over every rostered player who missed to injury or suspension (starter or bench, not a bye), of his expected-if-healthy points (recent scoring baseline, floored at 0). Year/all-time/league sum the weekly values.
Notes: Higher = more production knocked out (not schedule strength). Byes never count. 'Starter-adjusted Hardship' weights each by likelihood of starting; both feed Luck.

Formula: -1 × (standings_place − (pf_place + maxpf_place) / 2).
Notes: Negative when a team finishes better than their PF / Max PF percentile would predict (over-performance via luck or close-game wins).

Formula: Sample-size-shrunk mean O-Score of the picks the team MADE (picks.Team — the roster that made the selection, formerly 'Final Team'): skill = (n·mean + 5·50)/(n+5), pulled toward the league-neutral 50. Per (Team, Year) and all-time.
Notes: Higher = better drafting. The shrinkage keeps a 2-great-pick manager from out-ranking a 25-solid one. Blank with no made picks; vet / blank-O-Score picks drop out of the mean.

Formula: Sample-size-shrunk mean O-Score of the trades the team was in (trades.Team): skill = (n·mean + 5·50)/(n+5).
Notes: Higher = better trading. Blank with no trades; one-sided / untracked trades with a blank O-Score drop out.

Formula: Sample-size-shrunk WEIGHTED-mean O-Score of the team's waiver/FA transactions (transactions.Team): skill = (Σw·O-Score + 5·50)/(Σw + 5). Adds and swaps carry weight 1; pure drops (drop-only rows) carry weight 1/3, so they still register but a flurry of drops can't dominate.
Notes: Higher = better pickups. Blank with no transactions. Pure-drop moves ARE included, weighted 1/3 vs adds/swaps; only blank-O-Score moves drop out entirely.

Formula: Schedule-luck-free win rate: each week, score the team against EVERY other team (not just its actual opponent). Win % = (Σ over weeks of teams with strictly lower PF that week) / (Σ over weeks of other teams). Per (Team, Year) on team_year; pooled over all weeks on team_all_time. Ties count as neither a win nor a loss but stay in the denominator (so they depress the rate). Format 0–1, 4 decimals like Win %.
Notes: Reveals true scoring strength independent of who you were scheduled against — a team can be top-half (the existing flag) yet have a mediocre all-play %, or vice versa. Blank for a (team, year) with no games (e.g. the not-yet-played current/future season).

Formula: All-play win % − actual Win % (team_all_time uses 'All time win %'). Positive = the team's actual record UNDER-shot its all-play scoring strength (unlucky schedule / lost close games); negative = the record OVER-shot it (lucky schedule / won close games).
Notes: A compact schedule-luck read. Blank when all-play % is blank (no games).

Formula: Clutch index (PF): the team's average points-for per game in the WINNERS'-bracket playoffs (Semifinal / Final / 3rd Place) minus its average in the regular season, all-time. Positive = raises their scoring in the playoffs.
Notes: team_all_time only (#9). Blank for a team that never reached the winners' bracket. The toilet/consolation bracket is excluded.

Formula: Clutch index (win %): the team's win rate in the winners'-bracket playoffs minus its regular-season win rate, all-time. Positive = wins more in the playoffs.
Notes: team_all_time only (#9). Blank for a team that never made the winners' bracket.

Formula: TRUE when a LOSS would have flipped to a win had the team's hurt would-be-starters been available. Build a lineup from the ACTUAL STARTERS (real points) PLUS the injured/suspended players who missed (subbed in at their starter-adjusted hardship value), take the best valid lineup (bounded to lineup slots), and flag if that score beats the opponent's actual PF.
Notes: Healthy BENCH players are excluded — this asks 'what if their hurt guys were available', not 'what if they'd start/sat optimally'. Injury + suspension, never byes. Each flagged week also subtracts 0.25 from that week's Luck.

Formula: Count of 'Loss from hardship?' weeks per (Team, Year) / per Team all-time.
Notes: Blank for a (team, year) with no games; a real 0 stays 0.

Formula: The bye analog of 'Loss from hardship?', but SYMMETRIC across the matchup: TRUE when a LOSS would have flipped to a win in a world where NEITHER team had byes. For each team compute a bye GAIN = optimal(actual starters + its on-bye would-be-starters at their starter-adjusted bye value) − optimal(actual starters); add each side's gain to its ACTUAL score, and flag when this team's PF + its bye gain beats the opponent's PF + the opponent's bye gain.
Notes: Unlike 'Loss from hardship?' (a one-sided injury counterfactual vs the opponent's raw PF), a bye is a shared schedule artifact — so both teams get their on-bye would-be-starters back, not just one. The gain is a delta added to actual PF, so a gap between PF and summed player points on either side (e.g. the home-field-advantage bonus) can't manufacture a flip, and a bye-free week (0 gain both sides) reduces to PF vs PA. Bench players are excluded. Bye only, never injury/suspension.

Formula: Count of 'Loss from bye?' weeks per (Team, Year) / per Team all-time.
Notes: Blank for a (team, year) with no games; a real 0 stays 0.

Formula: The SYMMETRIC 'no-injury world' analog of 'Loss from bye?', for injuries/suspensions: TRUE when a LOSS would have flipped to a win once BOTH teams are healed. For each team compute a hardship GAIN = optimal(actual starters + its injured/suspended would-be-starters at their starter-adjusted hardship value) − optimal(actual starters); add each side's gain to its ACTUAL score, and flag when this team's PF + its gain beats the opponent's PF + the opponent's gain.
Notes: Differs from the one-sided 'Loss from hardship?' (which heals only this team vs the opponent's raw PF). Bench players excluded. Injury + suspension, never byes.

Formula: The mirror of 'Loss from hardship (2-sided)?': TRUE when a WIN would have flipped to a LOSS once BOTH teams are healed of injuries/suspensions — this team's PF + its hardship gain falls short of the opponent's PF + the opponent's hardship gain.
Notes: A win gifted by the opponent sitting more injured/suspended starter value than this team. Injury + suspension, never byes.

Formula: Count of 'Loss from hardship (2-sided)?' weeks per (Team, Year) / per Team all-time.
Notes: Blank for a (team, year) with no games; a real 0 stays 0. The two-sided complement to the one-sided 'Losses from hardship'.

Formula: Count of 'Win from hardship (2-sided)?' weeks per (Team, Year) / per Team all-time — i.e. how many of the team's wins would be losses in a two-sided no-injury world.
Notes: Blank for a (team, year) with no games; a real 0 stays 0.

Formula: The mirror of 'Loss from bye?': TRUE when a WIN would have flipped to a LOSS once BOTH teams' on-bye would-be-starters are restored — this team's PF + its bye gain falls short of the opponent's PF + the opponent's bye gain.
Notes: Symmetric like 'Loss from bye?'. A win gifted by the opponent sitting more bye-week starter value than this team. Bye only, never injury/suspension.

Formula: Count of 'Win from bye?' weeks per (Team, Year) / per Team all-time — i.e. how many of the team's wins would be losses in a no-bye world.
Notes: Blank for a (team, year) with no games; a real 0 stays 0.

Formula: Standard deviation of the player's points over his STARTED weeks only (bench weeks excluded).
Notes: Blank for a player who never started, and with fewer than 2 started weeks. player_year per season; player_all_time pools every started week.

Formula: The lowest single started-week points the player scored (absolute minimum over started weeks, not a percentile).
Notes: Blank for a player who never started.

Formula: The highest single started-week points the player scored (absolute maximum over started weeks).
Notes: Blank for a player who never started.

Formula: Share of the player's ACTIVE started weeks whose score landed in the BOOM tier — the top 10% (>= p90) of the ALL-TIME pooled distribution of active starter scores for that player's position (each week judged against its own position's bars). Position-relative, so it's comparable across positions.
Notes: The 5 Starter tier shares (boom / upper quartile / middle 50% / lower quartile / bust) sum to ~100%. Keeps a real 0 for a player who started but never boomed; blank if he never had an active start.

Formula: Share of the player's active started weeks in the UPPER-QUARTILE tier — between the position's p75 and p90 all-time starter scores (above the middle 50%, below a boom).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no active starts.

Formula: Share of the player's active started weeks in the MIDDLE-50% tier — between the position's p25 and p75 all-time starter scores (the interquartile range).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no active starts.

Formula: Share of the player's active started weeks in the LOWER-QUARTILE tier — between the position's p10 and p25 all-time starter scores (below the middle 50%, above a bust).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no active starts.

Formula: Share of the player's ACTIVE started weeks whose score landed in the BUST tier — the bottom 10% (<= p10) of the position's all-time pooled active-starter scores.
Notes: Position-relative (a TE bust bar sits lower than a QB's). Keeps a real 0 for a player who started but never busted; blank if he never had an active start.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile (0–100) of Starter scoring volatility INVERTED — the steadiest (lowest volatility) player at his position = 100. player_year ranks within (season, position); player_all_time within position across all players.
Notes: #10. Blank with no underlying volatility (never started / <2 starts). Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Starter scoring floor — the highest floor at the position = 100.
Notes: #10. Blank for a player who never started. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Starter scoring ceiling — the highest ceiling at the position = 100.
Notes: #10. Blank for a player who never started. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Points Above Replacement summed over started weeks. Each week the replacement level = the mean of the BOTTOM THIRD of that week's STARTED scores at the player's position; PAR_week = his points − that level; Starter PAR = the Σ.
Notes: Value over a freely-startable option, week by week. Blank for a player who never started.

Formula: The mean (not sum) of the per-started-week PAR — average points above a last-startable replacement.
Notes: Blank for a player who never started.

Formula: Standard deviation of the player's points over every week he actually PLAYED while rostered — started OR benched — excluding bye/injury/suspension weeks.
Notes: Companion to the started-only set, including the low weeks a manager benched him for. Blank with no played weeks (and <2 weeks).

Formula: The lowest single played-while-rostered week's points (started or benched; non-played weeks excluded).
Notes: Blank with no played weeks.

Formula: The highest single played-while-rostered week's points (started or benched).
Notes: Blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks (started OR benched) whose score landed in the BOOM tier — top 10% (>= p90) of the position's all-time pooled active-starter scores (same positional bars as 'Starter boom %').
Notes: The 5 Rostered tier shares sum to ~100%. Real 0 if he played but never boomed; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks in the UPPER-QUARTILE tier (position p75-p90), same bars as the Starter version but over every played week, started or benched.
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks in the MIDDLE-50% tier (position p25-p75 interquartile range).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks in the LOWER-QUARTILE tier (position p10-p25).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks (started OR benched) whose score landed in the BUST tier — bottom 10% (<= p10) of the position's all-time pooled active-starter scores.
Notes: Position-relative. Real 0 if he played but never busted; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Where the week's score places (0-100) within the ALL-TIME pooled distribution of ACTIVE starter scores for the player's position — percent of that position's starter weeks scoring at or below this week. Drives the 5 scoring tiers (Bust <= p10, Lower quartile p10-p25, Middle 50% p25-p75, Upper quartile p75-p90, Boom >= p90).
Notes: Blank on bye / injury / suspension weeks (a 0 there isn't a real score), and for a position with no reference starter scores.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive STARTED weeks whose Positional scoring percentile put the player in that tier (Boom / Upper quartile / Middle 50% / Lower quartile / Bust). Terminal-encoded like the other player streaks: the length shows on the run's final week, 'In Progress' before, 0 on a started week in a different tier.
Notes: Bye / injury / suspension weeks — and benched weeks — are skipped (the run bridges across them; those cells read blank). Spans seasons.

Formula: Like the Starter tier streaks but over every PLAYED week (started OR benched): consecutive played weeks in that positional scoring tier, terminal-encoded.
Notes: Bye / injury / suspension weeks are skipped (blank; the run bridges them). Spans seasons.

Stat: % of starters in each positional scoring tier
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Share of ACTIVE starters whose score fell in each of the 5 positional scoring tiers — on the team sheets over the TEAM's starters, on the league sheets over EVERY team's starters league-wide — that week (…_week), pooled over the season (…_year), or pooled all-time (…_all_time). Each starter is judged against its own position's all-time starter-score bars; the 5 shares sum to ~100%.
Notes: Bye / injury / suspension starts are excluded (no real score). Blank for a week/period with no active starters.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Rostered scoring volatility INVERTED (steadiest = 100), ranked like the Starter percentiles.
Notes: Blank with no underlying volatility. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Rostered scoring floor (highest = 100).
Notes: Blank with no played weeks. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Rostered scoring ceiling (highest = 100).
Notes: Blank with no played weeks. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Weekly flag: TRUE for the team whose single top starter supplied the greatest share of its PF that week (highest '% of points (if starter)' among teams' top starters). One winner per league-week, ties shared.
Notes: Its top starter is that week's Captain.

Formula: Weekly flag: TRUE for the team with the highest total bench points that week. One winner per league-week, ties shared.
Notes: Mirrors the weekly-award pattern.

Formula: Weekly flag: TRUE for the team with the most injured players (starters + bench) that week. Needs ≥1 injury — a 0-injury week has no winner.
Notes: Ties shared.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the One-man army.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly flag.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team left the most points on its bench.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly flag.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the most injured.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly flag.

Formula: Weekly flag: TRUE for the single starter league-wide who supplied the highest share of his team's PF that week (max '% of points (if starter)'; alphabetical tie-break). The Captain's team is that week's One-man army.
Notes: One Captain per league-week.

Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was the weekly Captain.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly Captain flag.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team had the week's highest team PF — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team had the week's lowest team PF — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team won by the week's narrowest margin — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team won by the week's largest margin — does NOT reset between seasons. Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team was the week's most efficient team (PF / Max PF) — does NOT reset between seasons. Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team finished the week in the bottom half by PF — does NOT reset between seasons. Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team scored 150 or more PF — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team sat at #1 in the regular-season standings — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team made no roster moves (0 transactions and 0 trades) — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team beat this same weekly opponent head-to-head — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks the player was Player of the week (highest-scoring starter league-wide) — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED (length on the final week, 'In Progress' before, 0 when broken). Bye/injury/suspension weeks are skipped (the run bridges them; those cells read blank).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks the player was QB of the week (highest-scoring QB starter) — does NOT reset between seasons.
