Formula: Current consecutive wins WITHIN the season, as a running count each week.
Notes: Not terminal-encoded — a plain running streak that resets between seasons.

Formula: Current consecutive losses within the season, as a running count.
Notes: Not terminal-encoded; resets between seasons.

Formula: Consecutive wins carried ACROSS season boundaries (does not reset at year end).
Notes: Running count, not terminal-encoded.

Formula: Consecutive losses carried across season boundaries.
Notes: Running count, not terminal-encoded.

Formula: Consecutive seasons the team made the playoffs (top 4 by regular-season standings). ALL-TIME (doesn't reset) and terminal-encoded: the length shows on the final season of the run, 'In Progress' before, 0 when broken.
Notes: Season-grain (the season is its 'week'). A season with no games yet reads blank.

Formula: Consecutive seasons finishing ≥ .500 (Win % ≥ 0.5). ALL-TIME and terminal-encoded like the weekly streaks.
Notes: A current/future season with no games yet reads blank.

Formula: Across all opponents this team has actually played (≥1 all-time game), the maximum of its 'Win % vs <opponent>'.
Notes: Opponents never played are excluded. 'Team for highest Win %' holds the matching opponent.

Formula: Across all opponents actually played, the minimum 'Win % vs <opponent>'.
Notes: Opponents never played are excluded so 'lowest' isn't trivially 0.

Formula: The opponent handle against whom this team's all-time Win % is highest.
Notes: Links to that team's team_all_time row in the xlsx.

Formula: The opponent handle against whom this team's all-time Win % is lowest.
Notes: Among opponents actually played.

Formula: team_week: 1 if the team won while its pregame avg Max PF was below the opponent's (an upset win). League sheets sum those flags (week = upsets that week; year/all-time = total upsets).
Notes: Formerly duplicated on the league sheets as 'Number of wins with pregame avg max PF from opponent' — that redundant column was removed; UPST is the single source.

Formula: League-wide sum of team_week 'Number of starter donuts' (started players who scored exactly 0).
Notes: Companion to 'Number of donuts' (all rostered players). Starter-only version. Every started 0 counts — you fielded them, so bye / injury / suspension is not excluded (unlike the bench portion of 'Number of donuts').

Formula: Max single-starter fantasy score league-wide over the period (week / season / all-time).
Notes: 'Difference between highest and lowest starters' is the gap to the lowest.

Formula: Min single-starter fantasy score league-wide over the period.
Notes: Can be negative (a started QB with a net-negative game).

Formula: DISTINCT trade events (by timestamp) the team / league was in that were dated BEFORE that season's kickoff (Sept 7). Each trade counts once regardless of how many teams were involved.
Notes: Year sheets per season; all-time sums across seasons (a trade lives in one season). Replaces the per-team 'Number of trades' which multi-counted multi-team trades.

Formula: DISTINCT trade events on/after that season's kickoff. Counted once per trade.
Notes: Year per season; all-time sums.

Formula: Offseason trades + Inseason trades — all distinct trade events the team / league was in.
Notes: Each multi-team trade counts once.

Formula: The fantasy team (manager) that rostered the player for the most time over the period. Reconciles transaction-tenure duration with the player's rostered weeks in player_week, so a teleport (a roster carryover with no transaction, e.g. a season-boundary carry) still counts toward the team that actually held him longest.
Notes: Manager name; links to its team_all_time row. The player_week reconciliation was added to stop an early, short tenure from outranking a long teleport tenure (Daniel Jones read shmuel256 for JacobRosenzweig).

Formula: Alias of 'Top team' (capitalised header on player_year): the manager who rostered the player longest over the season.
Notes: Same definition as 'Top team'.

Formula: The fantasy team that held the player at the END of the season (in-season window only, so an offseason move doesn't overwrite it). All-time reconciles the last tenure event with player_week: a tenure event that predates the player's last rostered season is treated as stale, so the team he was actually on most recently wins (a teleported player isn't stuck on an old team).
Notes: Manager name; links to its team_all_time row.

Formula: The player's age in decimal years as of that week (birth_date vs the week's date). Per-season = mean weekly age (≈ mid-season).
Notes: Anchored to the week, not today. Blank with no birth_date.

Formula: TRUE in the player's rookie NFL season.
TRUE when the player was rostered, scored 0, didn't play, and it wasn't a bye or suspension. From the in-house weekly injury tracker, backed by nflverse.
Notes: A player who scored > 0 is never flagged. Suspension wins over injury; bye wins over both.

Formula: TRUE when the player was serving an NFL suspension that week.
Notes: Takes precedence over Injury?; excluded from Hardship.

Formula: TRUE when the player had no NFL game that week — his team was on bye, or he had no active NFL team at all (retired / unsigned, NFL team = 'NFL').
Notes: Wins over Injury? and Suspension?; excluded from Hardship. The no-team clause keeps retired meme-pickups out of injury counts.

Formula: How much of his team's starting score a started player provided. player points / team PF; blank for bench players.
Notes: Drives the weekly Captain / One-man army awards.

Formula: This week's points minus his prior PLAYED week's points.
Notes: Blank on bye/injury/suspension weeks and in his first played week; the next played week bridges the gap.

Formula: This week's points minus the average of his previous 5 PLAYED weeks — is he heating up or cooling off?
Notes: Non-played weeks are excluded from the baseline and are themselves blank.

Stat: Change from career average to that point
Formula: This week's points minus his career PPG through the prior week (played weeks only).
Notes: Blank on bye/injury/suspension weeks and in his very first career week.

Formula: This week's points minus his full-career PPG (all played weeks).
Notes: Non-played weeks are excluded from the baseline and are themselves blank.

Formula: Running count of consecutive weeks the player has been on his current team, through this week.
Notes: Resets when he joins a new team.

Formula: A player's whole tenure with one team, terminal-encoded (value on his last week there, 'In Progress' before) so a sort ranks each player-team run once. '% of starts' = started / rostered weeks; '% of team points' = his starter points / the team's starter PF (0-1); the rest are weeks, points, and PPG as a starter or on the bench, and weeks as the team's top/bottom starter.
Notes: Separate stints with the same team share one tenure. Each column has a '… this season' sibling that resets per year.

Formula: Season / career totals across all teams. Rostered = started + benched weeks; '% of starts' = started / rostered; 'Total points as starter' = his starter points; '% of league points' = those points / the league's whole starter scoring (0-1).
Notes: 'Weeks as starter', 'PPG starter', and 'Times as Highest/Lowest starter on team?' are the other non-team versions.

Stat: Number of consecutive weeks on bench before start (if starter)
Formula: On a week he STARTS, how many weeks in a row he sat on the bench just beforehand.
Notes: Only populated on a starting week.

Stat: Number of consecutive weeks on bench before start excluding injury/bye (if starter)
Formula: Same as the previous column, but weeks he was unavailable (injury / bye / suspension) don't count as benched.
Notes: Only populated on a starting week.

Stat: Difference from best startable bench (if starter)
Formula: For a STARTER: his points minus the best bench player he could have started instead. Negative = a benched player beat him.
Notes: That bench player is named in 'Reference player name'.

Stat: Difference from worst benchable starter (if bench)
Formula: For a BENCH player: his points minus the weakest starter he could have replaced. Positive = he should have played.
The player the start/sit difference is measured against that week — the best startable bench player, or the worst benchable starter.
Notes: Links to that player's player_week row for the same year and week.

Stat: Difference in averages of best/worst startables over previous 5 games
Formula: The same best-bench / worst-starter comparison, but on each side's trailing-5-game average instead of this week's single score.
Notes: Smooths out one-week noise in the start/sit signal.

Formula: The start/sit difference combined with the handcuff signal — whether a much-higher-upside cuff was available.
Notes: Pairs with '- Activated Cuff?'.

Stat: - Activated Cuff? (Was a player of the same nfl team/position & who averages >10 PPG more over last 5 played games injured? Only for players with avg <10 PPG)
Formula: TRUE when a rostered handcuff (same NFL team + position, averaging 10+ more PPG over the last 5 played games) was started while its starter was hurt. Only considered for players averaging under 10 PPG.
Notes: Feeds the Cuff adjusted difference.

Formula: TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring starter league-wide.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as Player of the week?'.

Stat: Times as Player of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring starter league-wide.
TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring QB starter.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as QB of the week?'.

Stat: Times as QB of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring QB starter.
TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring RB starter.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as RB of the week?'.

Stat: Times as RB of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring RB starter.
TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring WR starter.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as WR of the week?'.

Stat: Times as WR of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring WR starter.
TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring TE starter.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as TE of the week?'.

Stat: Times as TE of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring TE starter.
TRUE the week the player was the lowest-scoring starter (no winner if 2+ tie at 0).
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as Benchwarmer of the week?'.

Stat: Times as Benchwarmer of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the lowest-scoring starter (no winner if 2+ tie at 0).
TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring QB left on a bench.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as Bench QB of the week?'.

Stat: Times as Bench QB of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring QB on a bench.
TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring RB left on a bench.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as Bench RB of the week?'.

Stat: Times as Bench RB of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring RB on a bench.
TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring WR left on a bench.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as Bench WR of the week?'.

Stat: Times as Bench WR of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring WR on a bench.
TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring TE left on a bench.
Notes: One per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as Bench TE of the week?'.

Stat: Times as Bench TE of the week?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring TE on a bench.
TRUE the week the player was his team's highest-scoring starter.
Notes: One per team per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as Highest starter on team?'.

Stat: Times as Highest starter on team?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was his team's highest-scoring starter.
TRUE the week the player was his team's lowest-scoring starter.
Notes: One per team per week (alphabetical tie-break). Count is 'Times as Lowest starter on team?'.

Stat: Times as Lowest starter on team?
Formula: Season / career count of weeks the player was his team's lowest-scoring starter.

Sheet: player_week / player_year / player_all_time / team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of roster moves (adds + drops) in the period — for that player on player sheets, for the team/league on team/league sheets. Rolls week → year → all-time.
Count of DROPS of this player in the period.
Notes: Rolls week → year → all-time.

Sheet: player_week / player_year / player_all_time / team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of trades involving the row's subject in the period.
Notes: On team/league year/all-time sheets, superseded by Offseason/Inseason/Total trades (multi-team trades counted once).

Formula: Mean points over every week the player was rostered here (started or benched).
Notes: Most derived metrics use the Adjusted variant instead.

Formula: Mean points per PLAYED week — bye/injury/suspension weeks dropped from the denominator.
Notes: What most derived metrics use.

Formula: The player's total NFL fantasy points that season — every NFL game, even weeks he wasn't rostered here.
Notes: From nflverse (re-scored with league settings), not LOTG rosters.

Formula: The player's total NFL fantasy points across his whole career — every NFL game in every season.
Notes: From nflverse, not LOTG rosters.

Formula: Per-game mean of Points (full season).
Notes: From nflverse, not LOTG rosters.

Formula: Per-game mean of the player's career NFL points. Points (full career) / career games.
Notes: From nflverse, not LOTG rosters.

Formula: Mean points in the player's STARTED weeks.
Notes: Blank if he never started that period.

Formula: Mean points in the player's BENCHED weeks.
Notes: Blank if he never benched that period.

Formula: PPG starter counting only played weeks (bye/injury/suspension dropped).
Notes: Blank if he never started.

Formula: PPG bench counting only played weeks (bye/injury/suspension dropped).
Notes: Blank if he never benched.

Formula: How much better the player scored when started vs benched. PPG starter − PPG bench.
Notes: Blank when he never started or never benched.

Formula: How many different managers rostered the player during the season / career.
Notes: Counts every team that held him at any point, even partial weeks.

Formula: Count of weeks the player was a starter over the period.
Win % of the fantasy teams over the weeks the player STARTED (a tie counts as half a win). Wins in those weeks ÷ started weeks with a result.
Notes: Requires at least 5 started weeks with a result in the period; N/A below that (avoids noisy small-sample rates).

Formula: Win % of the fantasy teams over every week the player was ROSTERED (starter or bench; a tie counts as half a win). Wins ÷ rostered weeks with a result.
Notes: Requires at least 5 rostered weeks with a result in the period; N/A below that (avoids noisy small-sample rates).

Formula: Season-champion membership flags. The champion is that season's Final winner. 'Rostered by champion?' = on the champion's roster at any point; 'Started by champion?' = started by the champion in any week; 'Started in championship game?' = started for the champion in the Final.
Notes: Booleans on player_year; the player_all_time versions ('Championships rostered by / started by / started in') count how many distinct title seasons each applied to.

Formula: Career counts of league titles the player was part of: 'rostered by' = # champion rosters he was on; 'started by' = # champions that started him in a week; 'started in' = # championship games (Finals) he started in.
Notes: Counts of distinct title seasons; the per-season booleans are on player_year ('Rostered by champion?' etc.).

Formula: Count of weeks the player was rostered, scored 0, and was flagged injured.
Notes: League sheets carry the league-wide total.

Formula: Count of weeks the player was rostered, scored 0, and was flagged suspended.
Notes: League sheets carry the league-wide total.

Stat: Change in points from previous season
Formula: This season's full-season total points minus last season's.
Notes: Blank for a rookie year / no prior season.

Stat: Change in avg points from previous season
Formula: This season's full-season avg points minus last season's.
Notes: Blank with no prior season.

Formula: This season's full-season total points minus the player's career baseline.
Notes: Blank with no career baseline yet.

Stat: Change in avg points from career
Formula: Whether the player is above or below his career rate this season. This season's per-game avg (full NFL season) minus his prior-career per-game avg.
Notes: From nflverse (every active NFL week). Blank with no career baseline or a season under 2 games (too small to compare an average).

Formula: TRUE if the player can be stashed on a taxi/practice squad: he's in his FIRST LOTG season AND has never started a LOTG week.
Notes: Resets at week 1 of his second season. A never-started vet from an earlier season is NOT eligible — only current-season newcomers who haven't started.

Formula: The team's starting-lineup points (points for) that week. In the Semifinal week the higher seed in each matchup gets +5 homefield, so its PF = Σ starters + 5.
Notes: Rolls up to season/all-time totals and averages; drives standings and Luck.

Formula: The opponent's PF that week — points scored against this team.
Notes: Drives Margin and Differential.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The team's best-possible lineup score that week from its rostered players.
Notes: Used for Efficiency and the optimal-lineup metrics.

Formula: How much the team won or lost by. PF − Points against (positive = won).
Notes: Summed over the period = Differential.

Formula: True for a win, False for a loss.
Notes: Feeds Win % and Record, which count every game (regular season + playoff/toilet brackets). A tie counts as half a win; none yet.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: How close the team came to its best-possible lineup. PF / Max PF.
Notes: ≤ 1; drives the Most/Least efficient awards.

Formula: The most unlucky loss: TRUE when the team LOST while being the 2nd-highest scorer of the week (only the top scorer beat it). A league in-joke.
Notes: Feeds the Luck model.

Formula: The luckiest win: TRUE when the team WON while being the 2nd-lowest scorer of the week (only the bottom scorer scored less). A league in-joke.
Notes: Feeds the Luck model.

Formula: Season / career count of the team's Brosenzweig weeks (unlucky 2nd-highest-scorer losses).
Season / career count of the team's Sisenzweig weeks (lucky 2nd-lowest-scorer wins).
TRUE for the team with the highest PF that week.
Notes: Ties shared. Count is 'Times Highest score?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team had the highest PF.
TRUE for the team with the lowest PF that week.
Notes: Ties shared. Count is 'Times Lowest score?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team had the lowest PF.
TRUE for the team that won by the smallest margin that week.
Notes: Ties shared. Count is 'Times Narrowest victory?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team won by the smallest margin.
TRUE for the team that won by the largest margin that week.
Notes: Ties shared. Count is 'Times Largest blowout?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team won by the largest margin.
TRUE for the team with the highest Efficiency (PF / Max PF) that week.
Notes: Ties shared. Count is 'Times Most efficient?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team had the highest Efficiency.
TRUE for the team with the lowest Efficiency that week.
Notes: Ties shared. Count is 'Times Least efficient?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team had the lowest Efficiency.
TRUE for a team with PF at or above the week's median.
Notes: Ties shared. Count is 'Times Top half of league?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was at or above the week's median PF.

Stat: Increase in points from previous week
Formula: PF minus last week's PF (Week 1 compares to last season's final week).
Notes: Rolls up to season averages.

Formula: How many roster spots changed vs last week. max(this week's roster size, last week's) − players on both.
Notes: Counts each swap once (not drop + add). Averages to 'Average weekly roster turnover'.

Formula: How many starting spots changed vs last week. max(this week's starters, last week's) − starters on both.
Notes: Counts each swap once. Averages to 'Average weekly starter turnover'.

Stat: Difference in pregame avg max PF from opponent
Formula: Pregame talent gap on paper. The team's trailing average Max PF minus the opponent's.
Notes: Positive = the stronger recent roster.

Formula: Count of the team's injured players (starters + bench) that week.
Notes: League/year/all-time carry the rolled-up versions.

Formula: Count of the team's suspended players that week.
Count of the team's players whose NFL team was on bye that week.
Notes: Byes are excluded from Hardship.

Formula: Count of the team's would-be-STARTERS who were injured that week.
Notes: Feeds Starter-adjusted Hardship and Loss from hardship?.

Formula: Count of the team's would-be-STARTERS who were suspended that week.
Notes: Feeds Starter-adjusted Hardship.

Formula: Points lost from injured/suspended would-be-starters, each weighted by how likely he was to start. The start-weighted version of Hardship.
Notes: Byes excluded. Drives the hardship-loss counterfactual.

Formula: How lucky or unlucky the team's result was, given how it and its opponent played — rewards unlucky losses / strong-but-beaten weeks, penalizes lucky wins. Gated by win/loss, with opponent- and own-strength, closeness, efficiency, and a hardship-loss penalty. team_year Luck = sum of weekly Luck.
Notes: Each loss-from-hardship week subtracts 0.25; calibrated so winning to talent nets ≈ 0. Full weighting in plan/LUCK_REWORK.md.

Formula: Win rate over ALL games that season — regular season plus playoff/toilet-bracket games. (wins + ½·ties) / games.
Notes: 0–1 fraction. 16 games in completed 2020, 17 in each completed 2021+ season.

Formula: The team's 'W-L' across ALL games that season — regular season plus playoff/toilet brackets.
Notes: Text companion to Win %.

Formula: The team's 'W-L' across every season (all games, incl. both brackets).
The team's win rate across every season (all games, incl. both brackets).
Notes: 0–1 fraction.

Formula: The team's 'W-L' in REGULAR-SEASON games only (the 'Week N' matchups; brackets excluded). Per season, or pooled all-time.
Notes: The standings record. Plain 'Record' includes the brackets.

Formula: Win rate over regular-season games only. (wins + ½·ties) / regular-season games.
Notes: Blank with no regular-season games yet (e.g. an in-progress season).

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' in championship-path games (Semifinal + Final). The 3rd-place game is separate.
Notes: '0-0' for a team that never reached the winners' bracket.

Formula: Win rate in championship-path games (Semifinal + Final), all-time. 3rd-place game excluded.
Notes: Blank for a team that never reached the winners' bracket.

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' in the losers'-bracket games that crown the toilet champion (Toilet Semis + Toilet Final). The toilet-losers game is separate.
Notes: '0-0' for a team that never landed in the toilet bracket.

Formula: Win rate in Toilet Semis + Final games, all-time. Toilet-losers game excluded.
Notes: Blank for a team that never reached the toilet bracket.

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' in the 3rd-place game (the two Semifinal losers). Small sample by design.
Notes: '0-0' if never played. Kept separate from Playoff record.

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' in the toilet-losers game (the two Toilet-Semis losers play to avoid last place). Small sample by design.
Notes: '0-0' if never played. Kept separate from Toilet bowl record.

Formula: Total points scored minus allowed over the period. Σ Margin.
Notes: Per season; all-time sums across seasons.

Formula: Average margin per game. Differential / games.
The team's 'W-L' in games vs that season's top-4 playoff seeds. All-time pools each season's seeds.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season (classes unknown until it ends).

Formula: Win rate in games vs that season's top-4 playoff seeds.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: The team's 'W-L' in games vs that season's non-playoff teams. All-time pools each season's classes.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: Win rate in games vs that season's non-playoff teams.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: The team's 'W-L' in games vs that season's champion.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: Win rate in games vs that season's champion.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' vs the champions, pooling each season's champion.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: Win rate vs the champions, all-time (pooling each season's champion).
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: The team's 'W-L' in games vs that season's last-place team. All-time pools each season's classes.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: Win rate in games vs that season's last-place team.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season.

Formula: The team's final finish that season — Champion / 2nd / 3rd / 4th from the winners' bracket, then 5th–8th for the non-playoff teams by record (PF tiebreaker).
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season. 2020–2024 finish folds in the toilet bracket (league rule then); 2025+ uses the regular season only. May disagree with the always-regular-season last-place stats.

Formula: The regular-season week the team was mathematically out of the top-4 race — the first week 4 other teams can't be caught even if it wins out. 0 for the 4 teams that made the bracket.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season. Regular-season weeks only.

Formula: Career count of league titles won.
Notes: In-progress seasons not counted.

Formula: Number of seasons the team made the top-4 playoff bracket.
Notes: In-progress seasons not counted.

Formula: Number of seasons the team reached the Final (champion or runner-up).
Notes: In-progress seasons not counted.

Formula: Number of seasons the team finished last.
Notes: In-progress seasons not counted.

Formula: Mean week-over-week roster turnover over the period.
Notes: All-time = per-season average, not a sum.

Formula: Mean week-over-week starting-lineup turnover.
Notes: All-time = per-season average.

Formula: How much a team churned its full roster during the season. Players who left PLUS players who arrived between the Week-1 and championship-week rosters.
Notes: Each swap counts as 2 (a drop and an add), so a full rebuild can top 40. Per season; all-time averages.

Formula: How much a team churned its starting lineup during the season. Starters dropped PLUS starters added between the Week-1 and championship-week lineups.
Notes: Each swap counts as 2. Per season.

Formula: How much a team reshaped its full roster over the offseason. Players who left PLUS players who arrived between last season's championship-week roster and this season's Week-1 roster.
Notes: Each swap counts as 2, so a near-total rebuild can top 40. Per season; all-time averages.

Formula: How much a team reshaped its starting lineup over the offseason. Starters dropped PLUS starters added between last season's final lineup and this season's Week-1 starters.
Notes: Each swap counts as 2. Per season.

Formula: How much draft capital a team used that season. Σ pick-slot value of the picks it made, on a 1/(slot+1)-style curve (earlier picks worth more).
Notes: Rookie-draft picks only. Excludes the 2020 startup and 2021 vet drafts.

Stat: Number of first round picks made
Formula: Count of first-round picks the team made that season.
Notes: Rookie-draft picks only. Excludes the 2020 startup and 2021 vet drafts.

Formula: Count of all picks the team made that season.
Notes: Rookie-draft picks only. Excludes the 2020 startup and 2021 vet drafts.

Formula: How sticky a team's roster is: of the players on its Week-1 roster in year Y, the fraction still on its Week-1 roster in year Y+3. |roster_Y ∩ roster_Y+3| / |roster_Y|.
Notes: Blank until the Y+3 season is played (2020→2023, 2021→2024, 2022→2025 so far). A player re-acquired by Y+3 week 1 still counts. All-time averages a team's measurable years.

Formula: Value of the picks the team currently holds for the next 3 years. Own retained + acquired − traded away.
Notes: team_week updates on each trade; team_year uses the season-end snapshot.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Total winning waiver bids over the period.
Notes: League sheets carry the league-wide total.

Stat: Change in win % from previous season
Formula: This season's Win % minus last season's.
Notes: Blank for a team's first season.

Formula: Mean of the team's season Luck totals, all-time.
Notes: League sheets carry the league-wide version.

Formula: Average points-for per game over the period.
Notes: League sheets = league-wide average.

Formula: Average Max PF per game over the period.
Notes: League sheets = league-wide average.

Formula: Average points allowed per game over the period.
Notes: League sheets = league-wide average.

Formula: Average game margin over the period.
Notes: League sheets = league-wide average.

Formula: Season / career total of the team's injured player-weeks.
Season / career total of the team's would-be-STARTER injured player-weeks.
Season / career total of the team's would-be-STARTER suspended player-weeks.
Season / career total of the team's suspended player-weeks.

Stat: Number of weeks missed due to injury
Formula: League-wide total injured player-weeks over the period.
Notes: League companion to the team 'Weeks of injuries'.

Stat: Number of weeks missed due to suspensions
Formula: League-wide total suspended player-weeks over the period.
Notes: League companion to 'Weeks suspensions'.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of QBs the team started that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of QBs the team rostered that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of RBs the team started that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of RBs the team rostered that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of WRs the team started that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of WRs the team rostered that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of TEs the team started that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of TEs the team rostered that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of started QB × started position-player (RB/WR/TE) pairs from the same NFL team that week. A QB started with a WR and TE from his NFL team = 2 stacks.
Notes: Counting stat — year/all-time sum across weeks; league sheets sum across every team-week. Free-agent/unrostered players (the 'NFL' team sentinel) are excluded.

Stat: Most number of players started from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's started players that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of players rostered from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's rostered players that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's started QBs that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Formula: The largest group of the team's rostered QBs that share one NFL team that week.

Stat: Number of NFL teams among starting players
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: How many distinct NFL teams the team's starting players represented that week.
Notes: Year/all-time = distinct NFL teams over the period; league sheets = league-wide distinct count.

Stat: Number of NFL teams among rostered players
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: How many distinct NFL teams the team's rostered players represented that week.
Notes: Year/all-time = distinct NFL teams over the period; league sheets = league-wide distinct count.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of rookies the team started that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of rookies the team rostered that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of handcuffs (cuffs) the team rostered that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of handcuffs (cuffs) the team started that week.
Notes: Year/all-time count DISTINCT players; league sheets are league-wide.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's rostered players who scored exactly 0 (a 'donut') that week — every starter who scored 0, plus bench players who actually played and scored 0.
Notes: A benched bye / injury / suspension week is an absence, not a donut, and is excluded; a starter's 0 always counts (you fielded them). Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored exactly 0 that week.
Notes: Every started 0 counts — you fielded them, so bye / injury / suspension is not excluded. Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's rostered players who scored under 10 that week — every starter under 10, plus bench players who actually played and scored under 10.
Notes: A benched bye / injury / suspension 0 is an absence, not a real low score, and is excluded; a starter's low score always counts. Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored under 10 that week.
Notes: Every started low score counts — bye / injury / suspension is not excluded. Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored over 20 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored over 20 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored over 30 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored over 30 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored over 40 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored over 40 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored over 50 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored over 50 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Stat: Difference between highest and lowest starters
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The team's top starter score minus its bottom starter score that week.
Notes: Week = per-week spread; year/all-time roll up.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Mean age of the team's rostered players that week (year/all-time = mean of the weekly averages, ≈ mid-season).
Notes: Age anchored to the week, not today. League sheets = league-wide average.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: How many of the players the team took in the inaugural 2020 startup draft it still rosters at that point.
Notes: Keyed off each pick's drafted player. Startup picks weren't tradeable, so the drafter is the pick's Original Team.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Of the players a team rosters, the share it drafted itself and has held ever since (startup, rookie, or vet draft). 100 × (drafted players, on their original stint) / (rostered players). team_week = that week; team_year = season-end roster; team_all_time = every player ever rostered.
Notes: A drafted player re-acquired after being cut/traded no longer counts (only the tenure that began at the draft). Waiver/FA adds and un-re-drafted ESPN-era players count in the denominator only. League sheets pool every team.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Same as '% of players drafted' but only for players in their 3rd NFL season or later (NFL season − rookie season ≥ 2). 100 × drafted 3rd-year+ / rostered 3rd-year+.
Notes: Rookie season = Sleeper draft_year (else current − years_exp). N/A with no 3rd-year+ players.

Formula: Total points scored league-wide that week (sum of every team's PF).
Notes: Year/all-time aggregate the weekly totals.

Formula: The spread of team scores that week. Highest team PF − lowest team PF.
Notes: Year/all-time aggregate.

Formula: How lopsided the week's slate was. Largest game margin − smallest game margin.
Count of games decided by 5 points or fewer.
Notes: Year/all-time sum.

Formula: Count of games decided by 10 points or fewer.
Notes: Year/all-time sum.

Formula: How close the seeding race came to flipping on the PF tiebreaker. The smallest regular-season PF gap between two teams who finished adjacent with the same record (so PF decided the seed).
Notes: Standings rank by record then regular-season PF. Blank if no two teams shared a record.

Formula: The deal's first counterparty — the team this team traded with. Links to that team's row of the same trade.
Notes: A 2-team swap fills only this; a multi-team trade also fills 'Team's traded with 2', etc.

Formula: The deal's second counterparty (only on a 3+ team trade); links to that team's row of the same trade.
Notes: Blank for a 2-team swap. One column per extra counterparty, so each can be its own link.

Formula: The player the pick was used on.
Notes: Blank / 'Unknown' for an unmade pick. Hyperlink target.

Formula: The drafted player's dynasty trade value on draft day (≈ Aug 28). KeepTradeCut superflex value.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a date before the player's KTC history.

Stat: KTC at end of rookie year
Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value at the end of his rookie season (≈ Feb 1 after the draft).
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a pre-history date.

Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value one year after draft day.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a still-future checkpoint.

Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value two years after draft day.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a still-future checkpoint.

Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value three years after draft day.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a still-future checkpoint.

Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value four years after draft day.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a still-future checkpoint.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in Player addition value
Formula: Did the pick beat what its draft slot usually returns? Its Player addition value − the average for that slot's comparison set. Positive = beat the slot.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in Avg PPG on team adjusted by position
Formula: Whether the pick out-produced its draft slot on the drafting team. Its Avg PPG on team adjusted by position − the slot's average. Positive = beat the slot.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in Avg career PPG adjusted by position
Formula: Whether the player's whole-career production beat his draft slot. His Avg career PPG adjusted by position − the slot's average. Positive = beat the slot.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in Avg points added adjusted by position
Formula: Whether the pick's started-week output beat its draft slot. Its Avg points added adjusted by position − the slot's average. Positive = beat the slot.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC on draft day
Formula: Whether the player was valued above his draft slot's norm at the draft. His KTC on draft day − the slot's average. Positive = above expectation.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC at end of rookie year
Formula: Whether the player beat his draft slot one rookie season in. His KTC at end of rookie year − the slot's average. Positive = above expectation.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC 1 year after draft day
Formula: His KTC 1 year after draft day − the slot's average at that checkpoint. Positive = above slot expectation a year out.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC 2 years after draft day
Formula: His KTC 2 years after draft day − the slot's average at that checkpoint. Positive = above slot expectation two years out.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC 3 years after draft day
Formula: His KTC 3 years after draft day − the slot's average at that checkpoint. Positive = above slot expectation three years out.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC 4 years after draft day
Formula: His KTC 4 years after draft day − the slot's average at that checkpoint. Positive = above slot expectation four years out.
Notes: Slot baseline = the 3-pick window around it + a 4th outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.
